# Privacy Policy

Last updated: April 23, 2026

This Privacy Policy applies to Cookle, a freemium recipe management
application created by Hiromu Nakano.

## Summary

Cookle is designed to keep your recipes, cooking diary, tags, and photos under
your control. The app stores the content you create on your device and, if you
turn on iCloud sync, in your Apple iCloud account through Apple's CloudKit
services.

The service provider does not operate a separate Cookle account system and does
not run a server that receives your recipe, diary, or photo content.

## Information You Provide

Cookle may store the following information when you choose to enter, import, or
create it in the app:

- Recipes, ingredients, categories, steps, serving sizes, cooking times, and
  notes.
- Cooking diary entries and meal history.
- Photos selected from your photo library, captured with the camera, or created
  with Apple Image Playground.
- Active cooking session state, timers, and recently opened recipe state.
- App settings such as sort order, notification preferences, subscription state,
  and iCloud sync preferences.
- Backup files that you explicitly export or restore.

This information is used to provide the app's recipe, diary, search,
notification, widget, Apple Watch, Shortcuts, backup, and restore features.

## Device Permissions

Cookle may ask for these permissions when you use related features:

- Photos access, to import recipe photos that you select.
- Camera access, to capture recipe photos.
- Notifications, to schedule optional recipe suggestion notifications.

Cookle does not ask for or collect precise or approximate device location.

## Local Storage and iCloud

Cookle stores app data using Apple's local storage frameworks. If you enable
iCloud sync, supported Cookle data may sync through your Apple iCloud account.
Apple processes iCloud and CloudKit data under Apple's own terms and privacy
policies.

You can disable iCloud sync in Cookle settings. You can also manage iCloud data
from your Apple device and iCloud settings.

## Apple System Features

Cookle integrates with Apple system features such as widgets, Apple Watch, App
Shortcuts, notifications, Photos, camera, Image Playground, Vision text
recognition, and on-device recipe assistance features where available.

These features may process your content on your device or through Apple's
system services. Cookle does not send your recipe, diary, or photo content to a
Cookle-owned server.

## Subscriptions and Payments

Cookle subscriptions are handled by Apple through the App Store and StoreKit.
Cookle can read subscription status so it can enable premium features, but the
service provider does not receive your payment card details.

## Advertising and Third-Party Services

Cookle uses Google Mobile Ads to show ads to non-subscribed users. Google
Mobile Ads and related Google services may process information such as device
information, advertising identifiers where available, IP address, diagnostics,
ad interactions, and consent choices according to Google's policies.

Third-party services used by Cookle include:

- [Google AdMob](https://support.google.com/admob/answer/6128543)
- [Google Privacy Policy](https://policies.google.com/privacy)
- [Apple Privacy Policy](https://www.apple.com/legal/privacy/)

Cookle does not sell your recipe, diary, or photo content.

## Backups and Data Export

Cookle lets you export a backup of your recipes, diaries, tags, and linked
photos. Backup files are created only when you choose to export them. You are
responsible for where you store or share exported backup files.

Restoring a backup replaces the current Cookle data on the device with the
contents of the selected backup.

## Data Retention and Deletion

Cookle retains your app data while it remains stored on your device or in your
iCloud account. You can delete individual items in the app or use the
data-management controls in Settings to delete Cookle data.

Uninstalling the app removes local app data from the device. If iCloud sync was
enabled, synced data may remain available in iCloud until you delete it through
Cookle or your Apple iCloud settings.

## Children

Cookle is not directed to children under 13. The service provider does not
knowingly collect personal information from children under 13.

## Security

Cookle relies on Apple platform security, device storage protections, iCloud
security, and App Store payment infrastructure. No method of storage or
transmission is completely secure, but Cookle limits its own data handling by
keeping user-created cooking content on device or in the user's Apple iCloud
account.

## Changes

This Privacy Policy may be updated from time to time. Changes are published by
updating this page.

## Contact

If you have questions about this Privacy Policy, contact the service provider
at [https://twitter.com/muhiro_12](https://twitter.com/muhiro_12).
